pr.title: Fix colored output of tools on Windows
pr.author: BR-

summary: Added optional Colorama initialization before Pygments terminal highlighting in two Python tools so ANSI-colored output works correctly on Windows consoles while remaining a no-op on other platforms or when Colorama is unavailable.
what_changed: In tools/decomp.py, before importing and using Pygments highlighting for --color output, the tool now tries to import colorama and calls colorama.just_fix_windows_console().
In tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py, before Pygments highlighting for --colorize output, the same optional Colorama Windows console setup was added.
Both call sites catch ModuleNotFoundError and continue silently, preserving behavior when colorama is not installed.
No decomp source, matching code, or generated context logic was changed; the patch only affects terminal presentation for Python tooling.
smart_moves: Used colorama.just_fix_windows_console(), which is designed to enable native ANSI handling on recent Windows consoles, emulate ANSI via Win32 calls on older consoles, and do nothing in other circumstances.
Kept the dependency path optional at runtime by catching ModuleNotFoundError, so colorized output can still proceed through Pygments if Colorama is missing.
Placed initialization immediately before Pygments TerminalFormatter usage, limiting the behavior change to color-output modes rather than affecting all tool invocations.
Applied the same minimal fix to both tools that emit colorized C output, keeping behavior consistent between decomp.py and m2ctx.py.
decomp_lessons: Cross-platform decomp tooling should treat ANSI terminal behavior as platform-dependent; Windows console support may need explicit initialization even when Unix-like terminals work by default.
Optional presentation dependencies should fail soft when possible, especially for helper tools where missing color support should not block core decomp workflows.
When multiple tools share a terminal-output pattern, fix all equivalent call sites together to avoid inconsistent developer experience.
Small tooling compatibility patches can improve contributor productivity without touching matching-sensitive source code.
naming_conventions: The existing CLI distinction was preserved: decomp.py uses args.color, while m2ctx.py uses args.colorize.
The patch reused upstream library names directly: colorama.just_fix_windows_console, pygments.highlight, TerminalFormatter, and CLexer.
No project symbol or decomp naming convention changes were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic was involved; this was a Python tooling compatibility fix.
The patch avoided changing any decompiled C, headers, symbols, or build outputs relevant to binary matching.
review_feedback: A comment noted that both colorama and pygments are listed in requirements.txt, but the ModuleNotFoundError fallback still allows --colorize-style behavior to work if someone lacks the optional packages.
There were no inline review comments in the provided PR slice.
searchable_terms: Fix colored output of tools on Windows
follow_up_queries: Search for other Python tools using Pygments TerminalFormatter without colorama.just_fix_windows_console.
Check whether requirements.txt consistently includes colorama and pygments for all supported developer environments.
Look for other Windows-specific terminal or path compatibility issues in tools/.
